body.title: BaseShim: health_surface() + check_circuit() hooks
body.result: BaseShim.health_surface()+check_circuit()+_post_status() added; GrannyShim override with relaunch_count+daemon; 17 tests green
body.description:
Both web UI tickets (T-web-ui-three-pane, T-web-ui-circuit-breakers) depend on BaseShim having two new hooks. Without them, the web server has no standard surface to query.

1. `health_surface() -> dict[str, str]` — returns key/value pairs representing this device's current state (e.g. {"active_sessions": "1", "last_dispatch": "T-foo"}). Default returns empty dict. Each device overrides to add its fields. The web server calls this on startup and on demand.

2. `check_circuit() -> bool` — reads the runtime circuit_state.json flat file, returns True if this device's circuit is OPEN (paused). Default implementation reads the shared file; no per-device override needed unless custom behavior wanted.

Also add a convention: devices can post key=value status by calling `self._post_status(key, value)` which writes to both health_surface() cache and to the device's channel (for the TTL-aged web pane).

**Affected files:** unseen_university/shim.py (BaseShim), unseen_university/skeleton/skeleton.py (if skeleton exposes BaseShim), tests for both methods
**Design rules:** OOP-first — hooks in BaseShim, never per-device standalone functions; no-sqlite; log circuit state read at DEBUG
**Scope boundary:** IN: health_surface(), check_circuit(), _post_status() convenience method; default implementations. OUT: per-device field definitions (each device implements those in its own override), web rendering (T-web-ui-three-pane), circuit UI (T-web-ui-circuit-breakers).
**Completion criteria:** `BaseShim().health_surface()` returns empty dict by default. A subclass that overrides it returns its fields. `BaseShim().check_circuit()` returns False when circuit_state.json doesn't exist or device circuit is CLOSED, True when OPEN. Tests cover both defaults and override. GrannyShim overrides health_surface() with at least one field.
